pm_decision_extract.reasoning_summary: DM surged 73% intraday from $2.23 to $3.86 on 2025-03-25. The surge occurred 4 trading days after earnings (2025-03-21) that matched estimates with no operational improvement (EPS -$0.28, revenue $44.1M). Fundamental deterioration is severe: net margin -97.37%, EPS -$1.07, free cash flow -$16.1M, debt/equity 2.04. Analyst sentiment collapsed from 13 ratings (Nov 2024) to 1 Hold (Mar 2025) — a hard-to-fake signal of deteriorating confidence. CRITICAL STRUCTURAL ABSENCE: No SEC 8-K filing is available to confirm a material catalyst event, and no news articles appear in the 7-day window (polygon_news returned 0 rows). For a NYSE-listed company claiming a 73% intraday move, the absence of corroborating SEC filings and news coverage is itself bear-thesis evidence. Wikipedia pageviews spiked to 197 on earnings date (2025-03-21) but normalized to 42 by decision date (2025-03-25), consistent with earnings-driven attention that faded, not a sustained positive catalyst. The macro regime (Strengthening / Moderately Restrictive) calls for strict quality and valuation discipline — DM fails both tests. Upstream agents: Narrative Agent (unsupported_claim, low confidence) found no concrete catalyst; Alt-Data Verify Agent (narrative_contradicted, challenge_thesis) identified structural absence of SEC filings, insider activity, and institutional positioning as bear-thesis evidence; Surge-Short Agent (valid short thesis) assessed momentum vs value and recommended short; Risk Agent (no hard veto) confirmed no mechanical veto conditions tripped. The preponderance of evidence supports a bear thesis: (1) severe negative fundamentals with no inflection, (2) analyst sentiment collapse, (3) structural absence of SEC filing and news corroboration relative to magnitude of price move, (4) macro regime discipline against low-quality names, (5) Wikipedia attention spike tied to earnings release (normal), not sustained narrative catalyst. The surge appears to be momentum/short-squeeze driven rather than fundamental value creation. Entry at 0.5% per §4.8 fixed new-entry sizing is appropriate.
